relevant_files.role: Main security scanner implementation, handles diff parsing and line-by-line scanning logic.
Pattern engine that compiles regex and performs the actual matching against text.
JSON database of vulnerability patterns currently using regex.
Data structures for PatternDefinition and Finding.
Integration tests documenting the multi-line detection limitation.

conventions.commits: Conventional commits (feat, test, fix) with scope.
conventions.testing: AAA pattern, integration tests in tests/, unit tests in modules via #[cfg(test)].
conventions.linting: Workspace-level lints (clippy::pedantic, clippy::all), unsafe_code warn, unused_must_use deny.
conventions.error_handling: anyhow for applications/tests, thiserror for library errors, tracing for logging.

related_issues.title: feat(security): evaluate tree-sitter for AST-based vulnerability detection
related_issues.relevance: Primary issue: proposes tree-sitter for multi-line detection.

related_issues.title: test(security): add negative test documenting multi-line detection limitation
related_issues.relevance: Merged PR providing the baseline test case for multi-line failure.

constraints: Must support multiple languages (Rust, Python, JS, etc.).
Must handle diff-based scanning (only scanning added/modified lines).
Should minimize binary size increase from grammar crates.
Must maintain performance for large diffs.
test_coverage: High coverage for single-line patterns; explicit negative test for multi-line SQL injection in security_integration.rs.

library_findings.notes: Core library for incremental parsing and AST querying.
Language-specific grammar for Rust AST generation.

approaches.name: Hybrid AST-Regex Scanner
approaches.description: Keep existing regex for simple patterns (secrets, weak crypto) and introduce a TreeSitterEngine for complex multi-line patterns. Use tree-sitter queries (.scm files) to detect source-sink relationships in the full file context of changed files.
approaches.pros: High accuracy, handles multi-line, avoids breaking existing simple patterns.
approaches.cons: Increased binary size, complexity in managing multiple grammars.

approaches.name: Context-Aware Regex Multi-line
approaches.description: Enhance the current scanner to buffer lines or use multi-line regex mode on a per-hunk basis instead of per-line. This would involve joining diff lines and applying regex across them.
approaches.pros: Low complexity, no new heavy dependencies.
approaches.cons: Fragile for complex syntax, doesn't truly understand AST, high false positives.

recommendation: Implement the Hybrid AST-Regex Scanner. Start by adding tree-sitter and tree-sitter-rust to aptu-core. Update PatternDefinition to support an optional 'query' field for tree-sitter SCM queries. Modify SecurityScanner to parse the full file content when a change is detected, but only report findings that intersect with modified line ranges.
